agent1_raw: The retrospective pilot has four confounds that prevent a causal interpretation. Self-selected activation, graduation-season timing, ATS integration selection, and staggered regional enablement together make the 34% lift uninterpretable. Weekly retraining with 48-hour label maturation is legitimate hygiene, not a leakage source. The interview-to-application rate is a valid KPI. The feature may work — but a randomized or matched test is required.
agent2_raw: Four interacting confounds: employer self-selection (opt-in during active searches vs platform average), seasonal overlap (February-May graduation peak), integration selection (ATS-connected enterprise accounts vs dashboard-only), and staggered regional timing (different start dates aggregated). The 41% vs 22% ATS split is the most telling signal — it reveals that integration tier and employer type are confounded with feature use.
agent3_raw: Self-selection, graduation season, ATS integration as a sophistication proxy, and staggered regional enablement all bias the observed 34% lift upward. None of these individually proves the feature doesn't work. But together they make the observational evidence too weak for a launch recommendation. A matched or randomized study is required.
synthesis_raw: Three agents agree on empirical_test_agreed. The four confounds are consistently identified. None incorrectly challenges the weekly retraining or interview-to-application rate as invalid. ETD: randomized or matched study controlling for employer mix, season, integration tier, and region.
all_issues_raised: Graduate recruiting season overlap: February-May peak
ATS integration selection: enterprise employer confound
Staggered regional enablement with aggregate reporting

empirical_test.condition: Randomized encouragement or matched difference-in-differences analysis
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Lift near zero or inconsistent across employer groups after controlling for mix, season, tier, region
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Durable lift of at least 10% within matched employers in same recruiting windows and activation tiers
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Feature helps integrated enterprise employers but aggregate gap explained by mix effects
